name: Electron
description: Electron — Framework for building cross-platform desktop apps with Node.js backend and Chromium-based renderer.

seo.title: Electron — Backend-as-a-Service
seo.meta_description: Electron: Electron — Framework for building cross-platform desktop apps with Node.js backend and Chromium-based renderer.

when_to_use: Building cross-platform desktop apps with web technologies
Distributing Node.js backend and web UI as a single desktop app
Desktop app for VS Code-like tools targeting developer audiences
Wrapping a web app into a macOS/Windows/Linux desktop binary
Cross-platform app with native OS features via Electron APIs

pros: Ship one codebase to Mac, Win, Linux
Huge ecosystem of modules and docs
cons: RAM and disk footprint are heavy
Not a backend platform at all
review.verdict: Still the pragmatic default for cross-platform desktop apps, despite constant performance complaints.
review.best_for: Teams shipping cross-platform desktop apps with web tech
review.not_for: Apps where native performance and small binaries are critical
